nodes.name: Telegram Trigger
nodes.notes: Download Files ON. Each uploaded .xlsx arrives as its own message, so sending 50 files = 50 independent executions (no name reply needed anymore).

nodes.name: Is it a file?
nodes.notes: TRUE = a document arrived -> render it. FALSE = plain text -> ignored (no name step anymore).

parameters.text:
⏳ دریافت شد — در حال ساخت و ارسال گزارش‌ها…
حداکثر ۳۰ نفر در هر فایل اکسل؛ برای فایل گروهی، گزارش‌ها یکی‌یکی فرستاده می‌شوند و کمی طول می‌کشد.

nodes.name: Send Working Note
nodes.notes: Instant '⏳ working…' note. States the 30-people-per-file limit and warns a group file is sent one report at a time. Runs on a PARALLEL branch: a Telegram node placed inline before the HTTP request would strip the xlsx binary off the item. Deleted again by 'Delete Working Note' once every report (or an error) has been sent.

nodes.name: Render PDF (VPS)
nodes.notes: One-shot: sends the .xlsx, the VPS renders every respondent to its own PDF, holds them under a token, and returns a JSON manifest { count, token, files:[{idx, filename, ...}] } — NOT a file. The PDFs are pulled one at a time by 'Fetch PDF (VPS)'. Stateless -> safe to run many in parallel (the VPS serializes renders itself). Set X-Auth-Token to your VPS token. onError continues so failures reach the chat; timeout 300 s matches the VPS render budget.

nodes.name: Render OK?
nodes.notes: TRUE = the manifest reports count > 0 -> fan out and send each PDF. FALSE = any failure (over-the-cap batch, VPS down, timeout — none of which carry a count) -> tell the owner instead of failing silently.

nodes.name: Split Reports
nodes.notes: Fans the manifest's files[] into one item per person. include=allOtherFields keeps the token on every item, so each report can be fetched by /file/<token>/<idx>.

nodes.name: Loop Over Reports
nodes.notes: Paces delivery: one report per iteration. With 'Pace (Wait)' the bot sends ~1 document / 2 s into the chat — comfortably under Telegram's ~1 message/second per-chat flood limit, so a 30-person batch (~60 s of sending) never trips it. Output 0 (done) -> clean up; output 1 (loop) -> fetch + send the next PDF.

nodes.name: Once
nodes.notes: The loop's 'done' output carries every item; keep only one so the working note is deleted a single time, not once per report (which would itself hammer Telegram).

nodes.name: Fetch PDF (VPS)
nodes.notes: Pulls one rendered PDF by index from the VPS. The token is read from the render node ($('Render PDF (VPS)').first().json.token) — it is the same for the whole batch and, unlike a per-item field, survives Split Out + the loop, so the URL is never /file//<idx>. idx comes from the split item. responseFormat=file -> binary 'data' for the Telegram node; Content-Disposition carries the Persian filename. Same X-Auth-Token as the render call. onError continues so one missing file can't abort the rest of the batch.

additionalFields.caption:
گزارش تحلیل انرژی شما آماده است 🌿
Chakra · Nazarbanai

nodes.name: Send PDF Report
nodes.notes: Sends one report as its own document — the 'regular format' the client asked for (no ZIP). .first() for the chat id: item pairing back to the trigger is lost after the split/loop. Retries once (3 s later) so a transient Telegram 429 self-heals, but the cost stays bounded so a systemic failure can't drag the run out for minutes. onError continues so a single failed send doesn't drop the remaining reports. Filename comes from the VPS Content-Disposition header.

nodes.name: Pace (Wait)
nodes.notes: 2 s between documents, then back to the loop. Telegram tolerates ~1 message/second to a single chat; 2 s leaves headroom so bulk sends (up to 30 reports) never hit flood control or a ban. Raise it if you ever widen the batch cap.

parameters.text: ={{ '❌ ساخت گزارش ناموفق بود.' + '\n' + ((typeof $json.error === 'string' ? $json.error : ($json.error?.description || $json.error?.message)) || 'فایل را بررسی کنید — حداکثر ۳۰ نفر در هر فایل اکسل.') }}

nodes.name: Send Error
nodes.notes: Relays the VPS error (e.g. 'batch of 52 exceeds the limit of 30') into the chat. The VPS returns the message as a plain string in $json.error; the typeof check also covers n8n-wrapped error objects, and the literal is the last-resort fallback.

nodes.name: Delete Working Note
nodes.notes: Removes the '⏳ working…' note once all reports (or the error message) have been delivered, so the chat stays clean. Uses .first() (not .item): the note lives on a parallel branch, so paired-item tracing can't reach it. The || covers both output shapes of the Telegram node.
